98728.default_location_instructions:
Merchandise Processing – Footwear & Accessories
• Remove packaging and shoe cartons; prepare shoes for display.
• Attach zip-ties and sensors to shoes in accordance with placement guidelines.
• Organize shoes by style before floor placement.
Ticketing & Sensor Tagging
• Verify ticket presence; check SKU information for missing or mismatched tickets.

Merchandising & Product Placement
• Follow latest floor set and merchandising guidelines for product placement.
• Place merchandise directly from Z-bars or cartons into the department while moving through the
store to reduce backtracking.
98728.default_position_instructions:
Freight Handling & Organization
• Unload and transfer cartons from delivery trucks to flatbeds, maintaining a maximum of six
cartons on the sales floor at a time.
• Keep cartons on flatbeds, when possible, to work at waist level and improve efficiency.
• Separate merchandise by category (apparel, shoes, accessories, home goods).
Merchandise Processing – Apparel
• Open cartons, remove packaging materials, and bundle apparel.
• Hang pre-hung apparel directly on Z-racks.
• Use the company-approved “flip” method for tops and “clip & flip” method for bottoms.
• Process two-piece sets by looping bottom hangers to top hangers and securing them together.

Promptly informs supervisors of any low stock/out of stock merchandise.

1. Stand directly in front of the shelves/counter (avoid angled shots)
2. Position yourself as close as possible while still capturing the full shelf
3. Do NOT exceed 12 feet distance from the shelves, even if it means the full shelf won't be captured
4. Capture as much of the section as possbile while keeping products clear and readable
5. If the full section does not fit in one shot, prioritise clarity over coverage
6. Enver there is no glare in the photos

Blurry photos will not be accepted.

37254.default_position_duties:
PLEASE NOTE THIS IS A LONG-TERM ASSIGNMENT. This assignment is 14 weeks long. DO NOT SIGN UP FOR THE SHIFT IF YOU ARE NOT ABLE TO COMMIT TO THE 14-WEEKS

* Assist with building and/or moving store fixtures
* General clean up and disposal of cardboard
* Collaborating with team members and working effectively as a group
* Capability to stand comfortably for long periods of time
* Strength to periodically lift up to 35 pounds

110298.default_attire:
- Clothing must be clean, not faded, torn, too baggy, or too tight; must cover tattoos.
- Bottoms: Khakis or pants
- Top: Retail Odyssey will provide a company polo. If you do not have on, wear a navy polo (black if you do not have a nave one.)
- Comfortable shoes
- Bring a boxcutter
- Bring a small pad and pen to mark out of stocks
- Bring a black marker
110298.default_location_instructions:
- This will be a consistent merchandising assignment where you are working packing out product for all DSD brands in this Kroger store. The program is run by Retail Odyssey, and you are to introduce yourself as a part of the Retail Odyssey DSD program.
- We are looking for a consistent merchandiser that will work at this dedicated store Friday, Saturday and Sunday each week from 12-6 PM. You will need to follow the instructions/survey in the app, and ensure you work each brand correctly.
- There will be a one day training on the first Friday where you will be training on the job and it will be determined if you are going to be a good fit. After that, you will be assigned to a store in the area to cover for the next 2-3 months. 
- You MUST have merchandising/retail/grocery experience, as you will be working these store independently.

110298.default_position_duties:
- This will be a consistent merchandising assignment where you are working packing out product for all DSD brands in this Kroger store.  
- The program is run by Retail Odyssey, and you are to introduce yourself as a part of the Retail Odyssey DSD program. 
- We are looking for a consistent merchandiser that will work at this dedicated store Friday, Saturday and Sunday each week from 12-6 PM. You will need to follow the instructions/survey in the app, and ensure you work each brand correctly. 
- For training, you will be meeting a supervisor at a store (which may not be the store indicated), and will be communicated to you the day before. 
- On this day, you will do training for a couple hours and then head to your assigned store to work on your own.  
- You MUST have merchandising/retail/grocery experience, as you will be working these store independently. If the survey/feedback suggests that you are not working the stores correctly, you may be removed from the program.

109906.name: Retail Odyssey - Louisville
109906.default_location_instructions: - Meet the team lead at the front check [stands.In](<http://stands.In>) order to clock in and your time to be counted:1) You MUST check in with the Retail Odyssey Team Lead. 2) Provide your full name to the Retail Odyssey Lead and say you are from Instawork 3) They will clock you in on the Retail Odyssey timekeeping system and give you the code to clock in. Make sure these times are exactly the same.4) When you check out, you will get your final time. You must match this time exactly with the time in the Instawork app. 5) Take a picture of your time in the Retail Odyssey system to settle any discrepancies. You will need this proof to change your time in case of dispute.IMPORTANT: You will ONLY be paid the official time recorded on the Retail Odyssey system. If you do not clock in an out on the Retail Odyssey system, you will not be paid. Match your time EXACTLY with the Retail Odyssey system. If your time is not recorded in the Retail Odyssey system, it will be considered a no show.
